title: Roles in the Process
description: Learn who owns what in the commercial process and how roles interact across teams.

sections.title: Everyone Has a Role
sections.content:
The Salesforce process involves multiple teams, each with specific responsibilities. Clear ownership ensures nothing falls through the cracks and every stage has an accountable owner.

Let's explore who does what and why it matters.

sections.title: Sales Representative
sections.content:
Owns: Lead follow-up, lead qualification, opportunity progression, deal advancement

Their Role:
• Verify lead validity and understand the customer's actual need
• Qualify leads against our criteria
• Create and manage the opportunity record
• Advance the opportunity through stages by gathering required information
• Shepherd the deal from qualification to closure
• Maintain data accuracy—ensuring the record reflects the true deal state

Sales reps are the primary driver of deal velocity and pipeline health.

sections.title: Account Manager
sections.content:
Owns: Customer engagement, account health, expansion, relationship continuity

Their Role:
• Focus on customers who are active or becoming active (while sales reps focus on closing new deals)
• Ensure smooth onboarding and customer success
• Monitor customer health, usage, and satisfaction
• Identify and drive expansion opportunities
• Own the relationship through contract lifecycle and renewal
• Partner with sales reps on complex deals to ensure proper solution design and support

Account managers ensure customers succeed and expand with NxN.

sections.title: CRM & Governance Team
sections.content:
Owns: Database health, duplicate management, data quality standards

Their Role:
• Review and manage duplicate records
• Set standards for field completion and data quality
• Define which records should be retained or deleted
• Manage historical data properly
• Support onboarding, training, and process enforcement

They're the keepers of system integrity and data reliability.

sections.title: Approvers & Leadership
sections.content:
Owns: Pricing approval, contracting governance, commercial control, pipeline oversight

Pricing Approvers:
• Review quotes before customer delivery
• Ensure pricing meets company guidelines
• Approve discounts and special terms

Contracting Approvers:
• Review contracts, amendments, and special terms
• Ensure compliance with company policy and legal requirements
• Validate signature authority

Senior Leadership:
• Own account ownership decisions for large/strategic accounts
• Oversee overall commercial pipeline
• Analyze win/loss performance

They provide the guardrails that protect company interests and drive accountability.

sections.title: Finance & Legal
sections.content:
Owns: Financial accuracy, compliance, contract protection

Finance:
• Review quotes for pricing accuracy
• Ensure payment terms are clearly documented
• Validate financial commitments

Legal:
• Review contracts for compliance and risk
• Ensure contracts protect company interests
• Validate regulatory compliance
• Advise on special terms and conditions

They're the last layer of protection before deals are locked in.

sections.title: Partnership Teams
sections.content:
Owns: Partner relationships, partner-led deals, channel attribution

Their Role:
• Own the relationship with partners
• Ensure partner-sourced deals are properly attributed
• Track and manage deals according to partnership agreements
• Coordinate between partner and internal teams

They're the bridge between NxN and our channel partners.

sections.title: The Handoff Matters
sections.content:
Success depends on three things:

1. Clear ownership — Each role knows exactly what it owns
2. Clean handoffs — Transitions between roles are explicit and supported
3. Shared visibility — Everyone knows who owns what at each stage

The rest of this training shows you exactly who does what at each point in the journey. No surprises. No ambiguity. No gaps.

keyTakeaways: Sales Representatives own lead follow-up, qualification, and opportunity progression through to closure
Account Managers own customer engagement post-closure, account development, and expansion opportunities
CRM/Governance team ensures data quality through duplicate management and field completion standards
